mission.title: Install and Configure Rancher / RKE2
mission.description: Rancher is an open-source container management platform that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters across various environments. It is ideal for organizations looking to streamline their container orchestration and management processes. Key trade-offs include the need for sufficient resources and potential complexity in multi-cluster management.

steps.title: Install the Rancher CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Rancher CLI, which is necessary for managing Rancher from the command line.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/rancher/cli/releases/download/v2.14.0/rancher-darwin-amd64.tar.gz

curl -LO https://github.com/rancher/cli/releases/download/v2.14.0/rancher-linux-amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf rancher-*.tar.gz && sudo mv rancher-* /usr/local/bin/rancher

```

> For macOS users
> For Linux users
> Extract and move the CLI to a directory in your PATH

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster using RKE2 with the specified version and node count.

```bash

curl -sfL https://get.rke2.io | sh -

sudo systemctl enable rke2-server.service && sudo systemctl start rke2-server.service

export KUBECONFIG=/etc/rancher/rke2/rke2.yaml

```

> Install RKE2 on the server
> Start the RKE2 server
> Set the kubeconfig for RKE2
commands: sudo systemctl enable rke2-server.service && sudo systemctl start rke2-server.service

steps.title: Verify the cluster nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in the Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> Verify that all nodes are in 'Ready' status

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
This step configures additional features such as monitoring and RBAC for enhanced security.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rancher/rancher/v2.14.0/manifests/rancher-monitoring.yaml

kubectl create clusterrolebinding rancher-admin --clusterrole=cluster-admin --user=$(whoami)

```

> Install monitoring
> Grant admin access to the current user

uninstall.title: Uninstall Rancher
uninstall.description:
This step removes Rancher and all associated resources. Data loss may occur, and orphaned resources may remain.

```bash

kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rancher/rancher/v2.14.0/manifests/rancher.yaml

sudo systemctl stop rke2-server.service && sudo systemctl disable rke2-server.service

```

> Remove Rancher deployment
> Stop and disable the RKE2 server
commands: sudo systemctl stop rke2-server.service && sudo systemctl disable rke2-server.service

upgrade.title: Upgrade Rancher
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up your data, drain nodes if necessary, perform the upgrade, and verify the installation.

```bash

kubectl get all --all-namespaces > backup.yaml

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rancher/rancher/v2.14.0/manifests/rancher.yaml

kubectl get pods -n cattle-system

```

> Backup current resources
> Drain the node before upgrading
> Apply the new Rancher manifest
> Verify that all Rancher pods are running

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates a problem with the kubeconfig or the server certificate.

**Fix:**
Ensure the KUBECONFIG is set correctly and the server certificate is valid.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the image repository is unreachable or credentials are incorrect.

**Fix:**
Check the image repository URL and ensure proper credentials are configured.

troubleshooting.title: Node status shows 'NotReady'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen due to network issues or insufficient resources on the node.

**Fix:**
Check the node's resource usage and network connectivity.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create resource'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may indicate insufficient permissions or misconfigured RBAC.

**Fix:**
Review RBAC settings and ensure the user has the necessary permissions.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.30, improved Helm chart management.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for Kubernetes versions below 1.27.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v2.13.x, ensure to update your Helm charts to the latest version.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get nodes' showing all nodes in 'Ready' status.
codeSnippets: sudo systemctl enable rke2-server.service && sudo systemctl start rke2-server.service

prerequisites.description: Ensure your environment meets the hardware and software requirements for Rancher.
